title: crepis spathulata subsp. boetica
intro: Crepis spathulata subsp. boetica, also known as the rock hawk's beard or Boetica hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and roots of the plant are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Crepis spathulata subsp. boetica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and those seeking natural remedies.
